frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Dekho, main blame nahi kar raha, but aajkal Delhi ka mahaul theek nahi hai. Itni late night akele ya cab se idhar-udhar ghoomne se better hai thoda apna dhyan rakho, decent jagah raho aur kisi ko saath leke chalo.
B: Toh problem ladkon ki hai ya meri?
A: Practical baat kar raha hoon yaar, ladke raaton-raat nahi badalne wale. Humein jo control mein hai woh toh karna padega na.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their personal responsibility and normalizes male predatory behavior as inevitable. It uses protective, practical language to justify restricting a woman's movement rather than challenging male violence.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
